extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Analysis of the transitions: sent is set true ONLY by t2 (send_attempt), whose guard requires not opted_out — so at the instant sent becomes true, opted_out is false. opted_out is set true ONLY by t1 (optout), which atomically also sets sent := false. No transition ever resets opted_out back to false, so opted_out is monotonic (once true, stays true). Once opted_out=true: send_attempt fires t3 (sent := false, since t2's guard fails), tick fires t4 (sent := false), and optout fires t1 (sent := false). Hence no reachable state can have opted_out=true and sent=true simultaneously. Confirmed by exhaustive BFS over the full reachable state space (12 states, none violating) and brute-force enumeration of all event sequences up to length 7 (none produce a violating state).
